task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jurism application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: A standalone note is created in Jurism
task_completion.visual_evidence: A note is visible in the Jurism interface. It should appear as a top-level item in the central items pane (with a sticky note icon), not nested as a child under any specific legal case reference.

task_completion.description: The note contains the correct title
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Research Memo: Constitutional Rights Framework' is visible at the top of the note in the right-hand note editor pane.

task_completion.description: The note contains the required text about Brown v. Board of Education
task_completion.visual_evidence: The note editor pane contains the text: 'Brown v. Board of Education (347 U.S. 483) established that equal protection prohibits racial segregation.'

task_completion.description: The note contains the required text about Miranda v. Arizona
task_completion.visual_evidence: The note editor pane contains the text: 'Miranda v. Arizona (384 U.S. 436) established Fifth Amendment custodial interrogation safeguards.'

task_completion.description: The note contains the required text about Gideon v. Wainwright
task_completion.visual_evidence: The note editor pane contains the text: 'Gideon v. Wainwright (372 U.S. 335) extended the right to counsel to state proceedings.'

task_completion.description: The note contains the correct concluding sentence
task_completion.visual_evidence: The note editor pane contains the text: 'Together these cases form the foundation of modern constitutional rights jurisprudence.'

integrity.description: The agent used the Jurism GUI to create the note
integrity.visual_evidence: The note text should be visible inside the Jurism application's built-in note editor, not in an external text editor like Notepad, Gedit, or Nano.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing sqlite3 commands or python scripts directly modifying the Jurism database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a standalone note in Jurism. The note must contain specific phrases: 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Miranda v. Arizona', 'Gideon v. Wainwright', 'Research Memo', and 'Constitutional Rights Framework'. Key legal concepts ('equal protection', 'custodial interrogation', 'right to counsel') must be included in the text exactly as specified in the prompt.
